member.name: Mr Tanmanjeet Singh Dhesi
member.synopsis: Mr Tanmanjeet Singh Dhesi is the Labour MP for Slough , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Young Futures Hubs: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what recent progress his Department has made on establishing a Youth Hub in Slough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to ensuring every young person aged 16 – 24 has support and opportunities to enter work or education. We are investing over £2.5bn to help young people build skills, gain experience and progress into earning or learning through...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Animal Products: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent steps her Department has taken to help prevent the importation of low-welfare animal products.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government will not lower food standards and will uphold high animal welfare protections. The Government will always consider whether overseas goods benefit from an unfair advantage and any impact on UK producers. Where necessary, the Government will ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Lung Cancer: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent estimate he has made of the percentage uptake of lung cancer screenings for those invited in Slough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Uptake data is not collected or reported at constituency level.Slough is covered by the Thames Valley Cancer Alliance. Since the start of the Targeted Lung Health Check programme in the Alliance area, 97,462 invitations have been issued and 45,492 people ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Olanzapine: Lactose
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the answer of 14 November 2025 to UIN 87821, which 11 companies have olanzapine products which do not list lactose as an ingredient.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The search that was carried out in November 2025 for UIN 87821 listed 11 companies. Accord UK Limited has since cancelled, as of 31 December 2025, its lactose free olanzapine licences, leaving 10 companies from the original list: Generics (UK) Limited; No...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Wexham Park Hospital: Midwives
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent discussions he has had with Frimley Health NHS Trust on progress made on the recruitment of midwives for Wexham Park Hospital.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not discussed midwife recruitment with the Frimley Health NHS Trust. Decisions on the recruitment of midwives are a matter for individual National Health Service trusts, which manage their recruitment at a local level, ensuring they hav...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Pollution: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent discussions she has had with Slough Borough Council on improving air quality in Slough constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Environment Act 1995, local authorities have a duty to assess local air quality and to take action to reduce pollution where air quality objectives are exceeded.Slough Borough Council is working with local stakeholders to improve air quality thr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Pollution: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent steps she has taken with local stakeholders to improve air quality in Slough.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the Environment Act 1995, local authorities have a duty to assess local air quality and to take action to reduce pollution where air quality objectives are exceeded.Slough Borough Council is working with local stakeholders to improve air quality thr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Air Pollution: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what recent discussions she has had with the Environment Agency on the impact of local businesses located in the Baylis and Salt Hill ward in Slough on air quality.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Defra works closely with the Environment Agency (EA) at every level to assess its performance, ensure it is equipped to carry out its functions effectively, and deliver for the public and the environment. The EA has received pollution incident reports and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Extended Services: Berkshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent assessment she has made of the affordability of wraparound childcare in (a) Slough and (b) Berkshire.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We want families to access the childcare support they need and get help with the cost of childcare, so that parents can work and children have the best start in life. We are rolling out free breakfast clubs across the country to support families with work...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: PE and School Sport Partnerships Network
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether further guidance will be published on the PE and School Sport Partnerships Network for schools.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is currently procuring a National Delivery Partner to establish and manage the new PE and School Sport Partnership network.Once this procurement process has concluded, we will be sharing further detail on how this new model will work. The N...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, whether he is taking steps to standardise medical discharge pathways for veterans across the Armed Forces.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Question 12263 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Greg Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12263
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Discharges
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what recent assessment he has made of the adequacy of support available to members of the Armed Forces who are medically discharged to transition into civilian life.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer I gave on 1 July 2026 to Question 12263 to the hon. Member for Mid Buckinghamshire (Greg Smith).https://questions-statements.parliament.uk/written-questions/detail/2026-06-23/12263
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Maternity Services: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent steps he has taken to improve emergency maternity care in Slough.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Frimley Health NHS Foundation Trust is the provider of maternity services to the residents of Slough, through community midwifery, Frimley Park Hospital services, and Wexham Park Hospital services.Since March 2026, a Day Assessment Unit has been in pl...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Fireworks: Thames Valley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent discussions he has had with Thames Valley Police on the prevention of the illegal (a) use and (b) sale of fireworks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No recent discussions have taken place with Thames Valley Police on the prevention of the illegal use and sale of fireworks. Enforcement mechanisms are in place to tackle situations when fireworks are sold illegally, or misused. Local areas decide how bes...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Fireworks: Slough
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what recent discussions he has had with Slough Borough Council on the prevention of the illegal (a) use and (b) sale of fireworks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No recent discussions have taken place with Slough Borough Council on the prevention of the illegal use and sale of fireworks.Enforcement powers exist for local authorities to take action when fireworks are unsafe, sold illegally or misused. It is for loc...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what recent assessment his Department has made of the adequacy of legislation in ensuring workplaces are maintained at a reasonable temperature during periods of extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Workplace (Health, Safety and Welfare) Regulations 1992 place an obligation on employers to provide a ‘reasonable’ temperature in indoor workplaces. There is a legal obligation under the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999 for all...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Bereavement Counselling: Parents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent steps he has taken to tackle regional disparities in access to specialist mental health support for parents following baby loss.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are committed to ensuring that all parents experiencing the devastating loss of a baby receive timely, equitable, and compassionate support, regardless of where they live.Every National Health Service trust in England has signed up to the National Bere...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The government consultation, special educational needs and disabilities reform: putting children and young people first, revealed the critical role that speech and language therapy plays in supporting children with speech, language and communication needs...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what recent assessment she has made of the availability of speech and language therapy for children in Slough.

writtenQuestions.heading: Autism: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment has been made of the adequacy of (a) resources and (b) support available to women diagnosed with autism as adults.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting women with neurodevelopmental conditions, including autistic women. All autistic people, including women diagnosis should have the right support in place, tailored to their individual needs, to live well in their ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

items.text: Name of donor: Malaria No More Address of donor: 85 Great Portland Street, First Floor, London W1W 7LT Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): Flights (£1912.12), accommodation (£964.07), parking (£178.00), internal transport (£835.61), visa (£140) and meals (£200), value £4,229.80 Destination of visit: Ghana Dates of visit: 28 July 2025 to 3 August 2025 Purpose of visit: To showcase how UK science is fighting malaria in Ghana. (Registered 29 August 2025)

Name of donor: Action Against Hunger Address of donor: Exchange Tower, 1 Harbour Exchange Square, London E14 9GE Estimate of the probable value (or amount of any donation): flights (£3,441), accommodation (£618), meals (£199), transport (£193), security and associated costs (£174), estimated, value £4,625 Destination of visit: Chad Dates of visit: 24 May 2026 to 30 May 2026 Purpose of visit: The visit aimed to provide an insight into humanitarian conditions in Chad, including the impact of displacement from Sudan, and to observe Action Against Hunger’s work, alongside that of other partners including UK-funded programmes, and to meet with a range of stakeholders and affected communities (Registered 23 June 2026)
